message:
Your dog's nose is basically a superpower — and one breed takes it to a jaw-dropping extreme. 🐶👃

Dogs have up to 300 million scent receptors (humans have around 6 million), but the Bloodhound is the undisputed champion. Their sense of smell is so precise that a Bloodhound can follow a scent trail more than 300 HOURS old — that's nearly two weeks — and stay locked on it for over 130 miles without losing the track.

It's so reliable that the trail a Bloodhound follows has actually been accepted as evidence in a court of law. 🐾⚖️

So the next time your pup buries their nose in the grass and refuses to budge, give them a little grace. They're not being stubborn… they're reading an entire story you can't even smell. 📖

Does your dog have a favorite sniff spot they simply MUST investigate on every single walk? Tell me about it below! 🐾
